info.title: arXiv Search API (LLM Optimized v2)
info.description:
Search and retrieve scientific papers from arXiv. This API returns Atom XML feeds containing metadata such as title, authors, abstract, and links to papers.

This specification is optimized for LLM tool usage and includes strict guidance for constructing effective search queries.

get.description:
Search for scientific papers on arXiv using a structured query language.

========================
LLM USAGE INSTRUCTIONS
========================

You MUST follow these rules when constructing queries:

1. ALWAYS prefix free-text terms with `all:`
   - Example: all:transformer
   - NEVER use plain text without a field prefix

2. Use AND to combine multiple concepts
   - Example: all:transformer AND all:neural network

3. Use quotes for exact phrases
   - Example: ti:"large language models"

4. For AI / ML topics, ALWAYS include a category filter:
   - cat:cs.AI (Artificial Intelligence)
   - cat:cs.LG (Machine Learning)
   - cat:cs.CL (Natural Language Processing)

5. Prefer relevance sorting unless the user explicitly asks for recent papers

6. If results are not relevant:
   - Refine the query by adding AND terms
   - Add or adjust category filters
   - Retry up to 2 times before giving up

========================
SUPPORTED QUERY FIELDS
========================

- all: search all fields
- ti: title
- au: author
- abs: abstract
- cat: category (e.g. cs.AI)

========================
EXAMPLES
========================

- all:machine learning
- au:"Geoffrey Hinton"
- cat:cs.AI AND all:transformer
- cat:cs.LG AND all:transformer AND all:neural network

========================
IMPORTANT
========================

- You MUST provide either `search_query` OR `id_list`
- Do NOT leave both empty
- Prefer `search_query` unless retrieving known paper IDs

parameters.name: search_query
parameters.description:
Structured arXiv query string.

LLM RULES:
- ALWAYS prefix terms with all:, ti:, au:, abs:, or cat:
- Combine terms using AND
- Add category filters for technical topics

GOOD EXAMPLES:
- cat:cs.LG AND all:transformer
- au:"Yoshua Bengio" AND all:deep learning
- ti:"large language models"

BAD EXAMPLES:
- transformer models
- AI papers

If unsure, default to:
cat:cs.AI AND all:<topic>

parameters.name: id_list
parameters.description:
Comma-separated list of arXiv paper IDs.

Use this ONLY when specific paper IDs are known.
Overrides search_query if both are provided.

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description:
Maximum number of results to return.

Recommended: 5–20 for LLM usage.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description:
Sorting method.

IMPORTANT:
- Use relevance for most queries
- Use submittedDate ONLY when user asks for recent/latest papers

200.description:
Successful response containing Atom XML feed of search results.

NOTE FOR LLMs:
- Results may include unrelated papers if query is weak
- Always check titles and summaries for relevance
- If results are poor, refine the query

schema.description: Atom XML feed containing arXiv entries

ArxivEntry.description: Simplified representation of an arXiv paper (parsed from Atom XML).
